revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 都祁山口神社 |画像 = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
山門（奥に拝殿） |所在地 = [[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁小山戸640 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 34 | 緯度秒 = 48 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 56 | 経度秒 = 45 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神 = [[大山祇神]] |神体 = |社格 = [[式内社]]（大）
旧[[村社]] |創建 = 不詳 |本殿 = 一間社[[春日造]]桧皮葺 |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = [[10月25日]] |神事 = |地図 = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|鳥居}}]] '''都祁山口神社'''（つげやまぐちじんじゃ）は、[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]都祁小山戸にある[[神社]]。[[式内大社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[村社]]。[[大和国]]十四所山口神の一座。 == 祭神 == [[大山祇神]]を主祭神とし、[[大国主|大国主命]]（国津神社祭神）を合祀する。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === 創建年代は不詳だが、[[神武天皇]]の皇子[[神八井耳命]]の御孫である都祁直が[[闘鷄国造]]となり、小山戸に居住し氏神を祀ったのを起源とする。元々は[[都祁水分神社]]と隣接して建っていたとされ、神社の背後の山上字御社尾に巨石があり、[[元慶]]3年（[[879年]]）に水分神が白龍となって降臨した場所と伝える。都祁水分神社は[[天禄]]3年（[[972年]]）[[9月25日]]に現社地（都祁友田町）へ遷座し、以後はその仮宮として関係を保った。在地氏族である都祁氏の衰退により社勢も衰微したが、都祁水分神社の仮宮として伝統を保っていた。 === 概史 === [[延喜式]]には大社に列し、[[月次祭|月次]]、[[新嘗祭|新嘗]]の官幣に預かっている。[[江戸時代]]には小山戸明神と称しており、[[近代社格制度]]では村社に列格した。 [[明治]]41年（[[1908年]]）には、大字相河の丸山に祀られていた国津神社を合祀した。 === 神階 === * [[天安 (日本)|天安]]3年（[[859年]]）[[1月27日]]、正五位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』）- 表記は「都祁山口神」 == 境内 == ファイル:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|拝殿 ファイル:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|山門 ファイル:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|社務所 ファイル:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|標石 == 参考文献 == *社頭案内板 == 関連項目 == *[[大和国の式内社一覧]] *[[闘鶏国造]] *[[都祁水分神社]] {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良県}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つけやまくちしんしや}} [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:大和国の式内社|大つけやまくちしんしや]] [[Category:式内大社]]

revision.text: Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara) is a [[Shinto shrine]].[[Category:Shrine stubs]][[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:new shrine redirects to break]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]]

{{Shrine | Name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | Image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | Location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | Coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | Deity = [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === Shrine Rankings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Major Shikinaisha]] [[Category:Images]] [[Category:new shrine redirects to break]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Major Shikinaisha]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]]

Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Major Shikinaisha]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:11]]

Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Major Shikinaisha]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)-qq]] [[Category:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[ja:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:trimming off this bad category]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:trimming off this bad category]] [[Category:qqqq]]

Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Major Shikinaisha]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:deprecated qq open]][[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqqqq]] [[Category:11]][[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qqqq]]

{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|大和国の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:Shrines in Nara City]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Major Shikinaisha]] [[Category:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[category:qq close]] [[Category:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:last 150 tagging]] [[Category:Sqqqqqqqqqqqq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:sqqqqqqqqqqqq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]]

{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja {{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|大和国十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi no Kami]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]

Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}}

{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|12=draft}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} (major) according to one theory, and its former rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=draft}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=draft}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=draft}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶|12=draft}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|12=draft}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|12=draft}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}}

In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] {{citation needed|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] {{citation needed|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}}

Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{citation needed|Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]

{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha shrines in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]

Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:translated pages without meta categories]]\n{{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]

In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|ja|春日造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasuga-zukuri|qq=}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[Nara City]], [[Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern shrine Ranking system|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern shrine Ranking system|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gankyō|ja|元慶|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gankyō|qq=}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|ja|天禄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenroku|qq=}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-no-Matsuri|lt=Niiname-sai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Niiname-sai|qq=}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]

According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[en:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]]

Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:奈良市の神社]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}

Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}
