revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}

{{Shrine | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}

In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Shrine↵" to "{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] "
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon.jpg|250px]]
Mountain gate (worship hall in the background) | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34|34|48|N|135|56|45|E|region:JP-29_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = [[Ōyamatsumi]] | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}

Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]]}} [[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:suge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]][[sn:Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine (Nara)[[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}}

It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|qid=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|qid=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]]
