[[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q59282579}}

revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

[[commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha [[:en:Kasuga-zukuri|Kasuga-zukuri]], Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]] was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]]’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in [[:en:Gangyō|Gankyō]] 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In [[:en:Tenroku|Tenroku]] 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and [[:en:Niiname-no-Matsuri|Niiname-sai]] ceremonies. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In [[:en:1908|1908]] (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * [[:en:Ten'an|Ten'an]] 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gankyō}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: Adding categories based on Wikidata P31: Junior Fifth Rank, Senior Fifth Rank, Shikinai Taisha, Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings, Kanpei-sha
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q59282579]] Wikidata name: Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gankyō}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gankyō}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu}}’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gankyō}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)]][[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gankyō|1=Gangyō}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku|1=Tenroku}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:都祁山口神社 (奈良市)]] {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579}} [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg | location = 640 Koyamado-chō, Tsuge, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}} | coordinates = {{Coord|34.58|N|135.945833|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|WD=Q386563|1=Ōyamatsumi}} | Shrine Rank = {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) theory
Former Village Shrine (旧村社) | Foundation = Unknown | Main hall style = Ikkensha {{ill|Kasuga-zukuri|en|Kasuga-zukuri|WD=Q3193781|lt=Kasuga-zukuri|1=Kasuga-zukuri}}, Cypress Bark Roof | Annual festival = October 25 | Map = Japan Nara | location = {{ill|Nara|WD=Q169134|1=Nara (city)}} }} [[File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Torii.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii gate}}]] '''Tsuge Yamaguchi Shrine''' (都祁山口神社, Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsuge Koyamado-chō, [[:en:Nara City|Nara City]], {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (major) according to one theory, and its former Rank under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|en|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|WD=Q712534|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was Village Shrine. It is one of the {{ill|Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja|Yamato Province 十四所山口神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fourteen Yamaguchi Shrines of Yamato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Deities == The primary deity is [[Ōyamatsumi]]. Additionally, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, the enshrined deity of the merged {{ill|Kunitsu Shrine|ja|国津神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is also worshipped. == History == === Foundation === The exact foundation date is unknown, but it is believed to have originated when Tsuge no Atai, a descendant of Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|WD=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}’s grandson, [[Kamiyai Mimino Mikoto]], served as the chief of {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} and enshrined their tutelary deity in Koyamado. Originally, it is said to have stood adjacent to the {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}}, with a large rock behind the shrine on a hill called Mikashirao. According to legend, in {{ill|Gangyō|en|Gangyō|WD=Q747451|lt=Gankyō|1=Gangyō}} 3 (879), the water deity of Tsuge Suibun Shrine descended to this location in the form of a white dragon. In {{ill|Tenroku|en|Tenroku|WD=Q1327229|lt=Tenroku|1=Tenroku}} 3 (972), Tsuge Suibun Shrine was relocated to its current site in Tsuge Tomoda-chō, and the Yamaguchi Shrine retained its status as a temporary shrine. Though the shrine declined with the fall of the Tsuge clan, it continued to be regarded as the temporary shrine for Tsuge Suibun Shrine. === Historical Significance === The shrine is listed as a major shrine in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}, receiving offerings during the {{ill|Tsukinami|ja|月次祭|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukinami-no-Matsuri|qq=}} and {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niiname-sai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} ceremonies. During the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, it was known as Koyamado Myōjin. Under the modern shrine Ranking system, it was classified as a village shrine. In {{ill|1908|en|1908|WD=Q2056|lt=1908|1=1908}} (Meiji 41), it merged with Kunitsu Shrine, which had been located in Maruyama in nearby Aikawa. === typeings === * {{ill|Ten'an|en|Ten'an|WD=Q1075611|lt=Ten'an|1=Ten'an}} 3 (859), January 27: Ranked as Shōgoi no Ge (Lower Fifth Rank, Junior) in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|WD=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}} under the name "Tsuge Yamaguchi no Kami." == Grounds == File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Haiden.jpg|Worship hall File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Sanmon2.jpg|Mountain gate File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shamusho.jpg|Shrine office File:Tsuge Yamaguchi Jinja Shahyo.jpg|Stone marker == References == * On-site information board == See Also == * {{ill|List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja|Yamato Province の式内社一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shikinaisha in Yamato Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Tōkenokuni|ja|闘鶏国造|1=Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|2=ja|3=闘鶏国造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkenokuni|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Mikumari Shrine|lt=Tsuge Suibun Shrine|ja|都祁水分神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuge Suibun Shrine|qq=}} {{Shinto side}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=奈良 Prefecture}} {{translated page|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)|version=69864234|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)}} {{wikidata link|Q59282579|co|commons:Category:Tsuge-yamaguchi-jinja (Nara)|ja|都祁山口神社 (奈良市)}} [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Yamato Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nara]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Taisha]] [[Category:Shrine receiving Tsukinami-sai and Niiname-sai offerings]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]
